codeReview.summary: Compared against the original failed feature review (fix-m3-nonce-prepopulate-test-hook): this rerun fix closes the exact timing gap by anchoring the 15s budget at dispatch and sharing that budget across request_id discovery and OperationFailure wait. The requestId-null path now fails fast when discovery exhausts budget, and A-side denial invariance assertions remain intact with no regression observed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture docs still describe a local A-side peer_denied runtime event as the denial-roundtrip mechanism, which conflicts with the contract/test reality used in this feature review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md (Data Flow: Approval Prompts) states denied requests emit local peer_denied on A, while validation-contract.md VAL-POLICIES-010 and policy-denial-roundtrip.spec.ts assertions focus on B OperationFailure + A pending/completion invariants and explicitly note lack of an A-side runtime event surface.

sharedStateObservations.observation: runtime-worker procedure requires full vitest + demo-gallery Playwright on every change, but scrutiny fix features scoped to e2e spec hardening are being validated with targeted checks only; this causes procedure/feature-contract mismatch.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/runtime-worker/SKILL.md step 5 mandates vitest + demo-gallery each run, while handoff for session 8105eab2-9eda-4bca-a709-5ac07a3fc376 verifies only targeted policy-denial-roundtrip Playwright + tsc and records this scoped deviation rationale.

summary: Pass: the dispatch-to-failure 15s enforcement is now end-to-end in the denial round-trip spec (including request_id discovery budget), and prior A-side invariance assertions remain preserved without regression.
